# v1 Slice Status

> Live tracker. Companion to [`_INDEX.md`](./_INDEX.md) (static backlog spec).
> Updated by `Plans/prompts/04-per-slice-template.md` (per-slice) and `Plans/prompts/05-parallel-batch.md` (parallel batch). Run `Plans/prompts/06-status-reconcile.md` when drift is suspected.

**Last reconciled:** 2026-06-10 (batch 251 reconcile — 657 webhookrecv first-class shared validation-handshake hook MERGED at `77e8a0e8` (#1243) (solo connector batch — REFACTOR; the LAST ready slice → queue now empty). Extracts the validation-handshake-in-adapter shape (Intune slice-557 Graph `validationToken` query-param echo → 200 text/plain; Azure Event Grid slice-522 `SubscriptionValidationEvent` body event → 200 JSON `{"validationResponse":"<code>"}`) into a reusable `connectors/shared/webhookrecv` seam: a `ValidationHook` interface (`Detect(req, body) → (response, contentType, ok)`) + a new `HandleWithValidation(w, req, max, hook, v, build)` entrypoint that consults the hook AFTER the size-bounded body-read but BEFORE the verify-first delivery path. Validation requests are unsigned and build no record; a non-handshake delivery returns `ok=false` and falls through to the UNCHANGED verify-first path → P0 verify-first invariant intact, NO bypass (orchestrator-verified the `Handle` wrapper is byte-identical: `Handle` now delegates to `HandleWithValidation` with a nil hook, so github/hris/pagerduty/mdm/eventgrid-delivery behave identically). D1 chose a NEW entrypoint over a functional-option variant (lowest churn, keeps every existing caller byte-identical). Both handshake adapters adopt the shared hook; the slice-557/522 tests are UNMODIFIED and green (orchestrator-verified zero existing `*_test.go` diff — only NEW test files added). INVARIANT #3 orchestrator-verified: zero `internal/api/`/migrations/proto/schemaregistry diff; receiver + handshake stay source-side, platform wire stays push; no new platform inbound API. `%q`-log discipline: the handshake path has ZERO log sinks; no validation token/code is ever logged → 0 unresolved CodeQL threads, CLEAN full Go matrix merge. Coverage: webhookrecv 98.9% (floor 96), eventgrid 98.4% (95), atlas-intune 86.7% (86), mdmwebhook 94.9% (94 — the engineer caught a `coverage-gate` ratchet drop on `mdmwebhook` from the new uncovered method and fixed it with a new test before push). No new kind, no migration, no spillover (the eventgrid `ValidationHandler` back-compat shim noted as a future cosmetic cleanup, not filed). The slice-656 shared webhookrecv abstraction now carries the validation-handshake seam as a first-class member alongside the verify-first `Handle`. With 657 merged the connector follow-on queue is DRAINED (658 stays not-ready/blocked on the write-scope-boundary decision) → GUARD-1 fires next iteration = clean terminus. PRIOR (batch 249 reconcile — 522 Azure Event Grid event-driven (subscribe) profile MERGED at `afa1726e` (#1237) (solo connector batch). Adds a `subscribe` profile + a SOURCE-side Event Grid webhook receiver to the Azure connector. SAFER-BY-DESIGN: the event is a TRIGGER, not the data — on a change event the connector RE-READS the changed resource via the EXISTING read-only reader (storage/aks/nsg/keyvault/firewall/entra, routed by ARM provider segment) + the UNCHANGED builder, and emits the matching EXISTING Azure kind; a forged/payload-only event or an unresolved id produces 0 records (no fabrication, tested). Delivery-key constant-time `Verifier` (header `Authorization` / query `code`, from `AZURE_EVENTGRID_DELIVERY_KEY`, never logged; mirrors slice-557 Jamf), verify-FIRST→401. `SubscriptionValidationEvent` handshake owned by the Azure adapter, intercepted FIRST → 200 `{"validationResponse":"<code>"}`, no record (mirrors slice-557 Intune). Cross-profile dedup reuses the slice-486 idem key `sha256("<kind>|<resource_id>|<hour>")` unchanged (subscribe key == pull key, tested). DoS-bounded: bounded queue (1024) + 5s coalescing window collapses storms to one re-read per resource; pull is the backstop. The 6 per-kind reread funcs were refactored into a generic `rereadKind[T]`. Built onto the slice-656 `connectors/shared/webhookrecv` WITHOUT modifying it (orchestrator-verified zero diff to shared/mdm/intune — the 656+557 byte-identical guarantees preserved). INVARIANT #3 orchestrator-verified (zero `internal/api/`/migrations/proto/schemaregistry diff; receiver source-side). No new Azure permission, no new kind, no migration. `%q`-log → 0 unresolved CodeQL threads. eventgrid coverage 97.8% (floor 95). PRIOR (batch 247 reconcile — 656 cross-connector shared webhook-receiver abstraction MERGED at `cce247d5` (#1231) (solo connector batch — first REFACTOR of the run). Factors the ~8 shared idioms out of the THREE independently-built source-side webhook receivers (github slice-044 · hris 573/655 · pagerduty 540) into a new vendor-agnostic `connectors/shared/webhookrecv`: `HMACConfig{Header,Prefix,Encoding,Multi}` + `Verify`/`Sign` (Encoding enum hex/hex-upper/base64); `NewServer` (gosec-G112 timeouts); `Serve(ctx)` graceful shutdown; `Handle(...,Verifier,BuildAndPush)` verify-first skeleton (method→405, size→413, verify-FIRST→401) + sentinels `ErrUnsigned`/`ErrBadSignature`. PRIOR (batch 245 reconcile — 655 BambooHR multi-employee webhook fan-out MERGED at `181ec7e2` (#1225) (solo connector batch). Closes a v0 correctness gap in slice 573: the BambooHR `subscribe` receiver acted only on the FIRST changed employee in a delivery's `employees[]`, so a bulk delivery silently MISSED the other terminations (a real CC6.3 leaver hole). The shared `PayloadParser.ParseWorkerID → ParseWorkerIDs ([]string)`; BambooHR returns every changed id, Rippling returns a one-element slice (single-worker path byte-identical, test-pinned); fan-out lives in ONE place. Partial failure: loop never aborts; missing worker emits nothing (not a failure); any error → 502 so the vendor retries (successes dedup-collapse via the unchanged hour-truncated idem key). In-delivery dedup + MaxFanOut=100. Signature verify stays first/once-per-delivery before any fan-out (forged 3-employee delivery → 0 processed, 401). Reuses `hris.worker_lifecycle.v1` + slice-491 PII guard unchanged. INVARIANT #3 orchestrator-verified (zero `internal/api/`/migrations/proto/schemaregistry diff). hris/webhook coverage 98.1%. **CodeQL/merge-gating note (NEW class of blocker this session):** branch protection has `required_conversation_resolution: enabled`, and CodeQL posted `go/log-injection` (CWE-117) review threads on the fan-out failure log (the webhook-provided worker id reached the log via BOTH the id arg AND the upstream re-read/push error wraps + the workers-client errors that embed the id). `Analyze (go)` PASSED (alerts post as review threads, they do not fail the check) but the unresolved threads BLOCKED merge despite all 11 required status checks green. Orchestrator-as-mechanic fix (root cause, NOT thread-dismissal): `%q`-format BOTH the id AND the error at the log sink — `%q` invokes `err.Error()` and quotes/escapes it (newlines → `\n`), which CodeQL recognizes as the log-injection barrier; first pass (`%q` on id + inner wraps) cleared one alert but CodeQL still traced the value into `err`→`%v`, so a second commit `%q`-formatted the err itself, clearing alerts 39/40/41 and auto-resolving all 3 threads. Also: one shard-A Docker-registry pull timeout (the `svc-container` infra-flake class) re-run clean. NOTE: `CI · merge-gate` still ADVISORY pending maintainer branch-protection update. Both existing adapters adopt the shared hook; their slice-557/522 tests stay green (externally-observable behaviour unchanged). The 656 byte-identical guarantee for the existing `Handle`/`Verifier`/`BuildAndPush` exported surface + the mdm/github/hris/pagerduty adapters MUST be preserved (verify zero behavioural diff). SOURCE-side (invariant #3 — platform wire stays push); no new platform inbound API; no new kind, no migration. `%q` any user-tainted value reaching a log. ## Reconcile — 2026-06-10 (batch 250 · 526 merged)

Solo connector batch. 526 K8s watch-based event-driven (subscribe) profile — slice 487 shipped the K8s connector pull-only. Adds the `subscribe` profile: a persistent WATCH consumer (NOT a webhook receiver — a different runtime class, so the shared `webhookrecv` does NOT apply) that reacts to RBAC/workload changes as they happen and emits the SAME two existing kinds (`k8s.rbac_binding.v1`, `k8s.workload_security_context.v1`) — no new schema. JUDGMENT: primary mechanism is a `watch` against the same read-only API surfaces (universally available; managed clusters often don't expose the audit log → the watch is the portable choice; document the audit-log option). The watch lifecycle is the load-bearing novelty: resourceVersion tracking, `allowWatchBookmarks`, and 410-Gone (resourceVersion-too-old) → re-list-and-resume. Reuses the slice-487 `rbac`/`workload` record builders + the slice-487 `idem` hour-window dedup UNCHANGED — a burst of edits to one binding collapses to one record (DoS coalescing). The read-only ClusterRole gains AT MOST a `watch` verb on the EXISTING resources (alongside `get,list`) — no new resource, never `secrets`, never a write verb, never a wildcard (least-privilege test updated). Config-only (the audit/watch event carries no Secret/ConfigMap/env/log payload — a no-leak test asserts it). `profiles_supported=[pull, subscribe]`, honest "event-driven via watch" naming (NOT "continuous monitoring"); pull stays the reconciliation backstop. SOURCE-side (invariant #3 — platform wire stays push); `%q` every resource-id/name reaching a log. No new evidence kind, no migration. Canonical row is `ready`; parent #487 merged.

Anchors CRY-01 + CRY-09 (both verified in the bundled catalog; spec-candidate IAC-22 confirmed absent → avoided dangling). Reuses the shared slice-621 `k8slist` reader. Pull-only, no wire change, no migration, no spillover. secretmeta coverage 98.0% (floor 96).

## Reconcile — 2026-06-08 (batch 225 · 615 + 620 + 633 merged)

- **633** (fix 474 ingest/verify hash round-trip) — **MERGED**. Root cause confirmed by byte-diff: `observed_at` sole divergent field (proto-nanos hashed at ingest, µs-truncated by `TIMESTAMPTZ`, lossily reconstructed at verify). Fix: additive nullable `observed_at_nanos BIGINT` (`UnixNano()`), reconstructed faithfully with legacy-NULL fallback to the lossy column. `HashRecord` untouched → cross-language client receipt-hash contract byte-identical. New host-clock-independent pure-Go round-trip guard (the regression net 474 lacked) + integration tamper assertion. Migration `20260608070000_evidence_observed_at_nanos.sql`. **Why 474 escaped:** macOS `time.Now()` is µs-aligned so 474's local run passed; CI-Linux (full nanos) went RED and 474 merged red anyway. Proven: shard A green on 633's PR.
- **615** (Azure KV RBAC role-assignment enumeration) — **MERGED**. RBAC-mode vault → second read-only ARM read of `Microsoft.Authorization/roleAssignments` scoped to each vault, merged into `access_entries` as `principal_type=rbac_role_assignment`. No schema change; structural metadata-only over-collection guard (no field for secret material); ARM Reader only. Spillover 623.
- **620** (map unmapped vendor claim → SCF anchor) — **MERGED**. `PATCH /v1/oscal/component-claims/{id}/scf-anchor` (approver-gated, RLS); invariant #7 (requirement→anchor only); no fabricated coverage (integration test asserts `control_evaluations` stays empty); audit via generalized slice-589 event log; web anchor-picker. Pure-pydantic bridge parser, no external href deref (socket-monkeypatch test). Migration `20260608010000_oscal_component_definitions.sql`; profileimport→componentimport sibling pattern. **CI-fix:** stubs regenerated at grpcio-tools 1.80.0 (engineer's 1.81.0 broke the bridge job vs CI's grpcio 1.80.0). No migration. No spillover.

Dedicated `Secret` type redacts through all formatting/JSON. Default-opted-out + claim-before-send idempotency per channel; migration `20260608000000_slack_webhook_channels.sql`. Spillovers 582/583/584.

## Reconcile — 2026-06-07 (batch 209 · 482 merged)

Solo full-stack batch — varied from connectors to close the framework-analytics thread. Merged CLEAN. **The canvas §3.2 headline promise now ships: per-requirement coverage STRENGTH is computed + visualized across the 5 frameworks.**

Additive `coverage_strength` (0.0-1.0) + `confidence_band` on `GET /v1/requirements/{id}/coverage`, computed server-side as best-satisfying-path (`MAX over anchors of edge_strength × anchor_coverage`; anchor_coverage = best 30-day effectiveness pass-rate over the tenant's in-scope controls) — single-anchor reduces exactly to the canvas 0.8 example. Bands: strong ≥0.80 / partial / weak / uncovered (a 0.0 score WITH an in-scope control = weak "covered-but-failing", not uncovered). **RLS-scoped — the load-bearing AC-7 integration test proves tenant B never sees tenant A's score (P0-482-3).** Freshness deliberately NOT double-counted (the 30-day window is already freshness-bounded; v2 revisit). Control-detail UI renders per-anchor STRM+strength AND the rolled-up band. Additive only; no client-supplied strength; no crosswalk-edit UI / matrix. 10/10 ACs, all 6 P0s. Decisions log: `docs/audit-log/482-coverage-strength-rollup-decisions.md`.

New `ImportCatalog` RPC on the existing Go↔Python `oscal-bridge` (extended, not a second bridge) + Python compliance-trestle impl (OSCAL v1.1.x validation, **no `href`/external dereference**, 16 MiB + 10k-control + 30s caps) + a Go `internal/oscal/catalogimport.Importer` persisting a **provenance-labeled** imported-catalog set (source=`oscal-import`, importer, source sha256, label, version, count) mapped **requirement → SCF anchor** (invariant #7, never req→req), **transactional** (nothing persists on failure), **tenant-scoped + `grc_engineer`-gated**, **audit-logged**, + `atlas-oscal import-catalog <file>` CLI (text + `--json`). 13/13 ACs pass; all 6 P0s honored. Migration `20260606010000_oscal_imported_catalogs` (distinct imported tables; SCF spine never written). Decisions log: `docs/audit-log/492-oscal-import-decisions.md`.

THE last invariant-#8 violation: §8 names both directions ("ingest catalogs/profiles/component-definitions; export SSP/AP/AR/POA&M") but only export is built — no caller anywhere in `internal/`/`cmd/` parses an inbound OSCAL document. ## Reconcile — 2026-06-07 (batch 198 · 495 + 473 + 477 merged)

First build batch off the gap-analysis backlog — all three merged. Two of the three real-defect slices' siblings landed (495 is the defect itself; 473/477 the prod-confirmed + fan-out fixes). Conflict surfaces held. Also cleaned a pre-existing CHANGELOG defect (#1044, 2 orphan `|||||||` diff3 markers from an earlier keep-all sed that omitted the base-marker pattern — flagged by 2 engineers; the keep-all sed is corrected going forward to include `/^||||||| /d`).

- **495** (control-as-code: evaluate SQL + JSON-path evidence queries, not just Rego) — Quality · JUDGMENT — **MERGED** at `f3f74bf4` (#1043). THE real defect fixed: the engine accepted `rego|sql|jsonpath` at upload but only evaluated `rego`, silently producing no state for SQL/JSON-path controls. New `internal/eval/sql.go` (read-only SQL evaluator) + `jsonpath.go` (in-process); engine dispatches ALL declared queries + rolls up; unsupported language now FAILS LOUD (was silent no-op). **The AC-10 cross-tenant test caught a real RED:** a CTE-only approach let author SQL reach the live `evidence_records`/`users` tables — sealed with an empty `search_path` + a static schema-qualified-ref guard + the READ-ONLY rolled-back subtxn. Coverage 73→77. No spillover.
- **473** (idempotent fail-closed migrate-on-upgrade for the self-host stack) — Infra/Deploy · JUDGMENT — **MERGED** at `8d5da2c0` (#1042, after a regression-fix recovery). The prod-confirmed fix: split a dedicated always-run `atlas-migrate[-edge]` service out of the first-boot bootstrap, gated `service_completed_successfully` (the bootstrap control-upload phase can't be so gated — slice-065 deadlock — but the migrate-only step can), fail-closed naming the failing migration. **Regression caught + fixed in-batch:** the first push passed the new `migrate` CI leg but broke the existing `bundled`+`external` self-host-bundle legs — root cause was TWO test/overlay plumbing bugs (an EXIT-trap `[ -n "" ] && rm` leaking exit 1 as the script status; the slice-470 proxy overlay missing `atlas-migrate`'s `atlasnet` membership), NOT the fail-closed gate. eng-473 reproduced locally + fixed both → all 4 self-host-bundle legs (bundled/external/migrate/proxy) green. AC-7 reproduces the incident (ledger 67→68 + serve-after-gate + idempotent no-reseed). Reconciles slice-464 migrate-prose drift. No spillover.
- **477** (walkthrough PDF render degrades to 503 under load) — Quality · JUDGMENT — **MERGED** at `4ce4733c` (#1041). The walkthrough renderer had the identical bug 475 fixed for board/questionnaires; routed through the shared `internal/pdfrender.Limiter` + deterministic 503 mapping; enrolled `internal/api/walkthroughs` in the shard manifest (its first test). Deferred a pure-Go `pdfDegradation` coverage floor (sibling 495 owned coverage-thresholds this batch) — candidate tiny follow-up, noted in its decisions log.

No spillovers this batch. Backlog: the ~15 remaining ready gap-slices (480/481/482 · 486-491 connectors · 492/493/494/496 · 474 · 478 · 508/509/510) + the older analysis tail. The other 2 real-defects 492 (OSCAL import) + 498 (LLM foundation) are next-priority. 478 (user-mgmt API, LARGE) merging will flip 479 → ready; 498 merging flips 499-502 → ready. AI-assist 440/441/444/471 maintainer-sequenced; decision-gates 446/455/PCI-CDE out of the loop.

## Reconcile — 2026-06-06 (batch 197 · 464 + 472 merged; + 475 unblock fix)

Eleventh drain batch. Both batch-197 slices merged — but only after fixing the recurring board-PDF flake that had blocked the merge queue (a mid-batch slice 475 build, maintainer-directed). The flake class is now resolved.

- **472** (oauth coverage tail toward 90% advisory) — Quality · JUDGMENT — **MERGED** at `f6cd0eec` (#1026, after rebasing onto 475). `internal/api/oauth` 80.8% → **89.3%**, floor 78 → **87** — ~0.7pp from the 90 security-critical advisory. Device-approval + DBUserResolver-authPool arms. No spillover.
- **464** (`atlas evidence verify` ledger-integrity walk + SELF_HOSTING migrate-prose fix) — Infra · JUDGMENT — **MERGED** at `c3d08cbc` (#1027, UNSTABLE-codecov-only, after rebase). Adds the operator verb to re-walk the ledger + verify per-record sha256 (the surface slice 432's restore-drill found missing) + a `GRANT SELECT` migration for the cross-tenant service-account read. **Surfaced a real integrity finding → spillover 474:** the ingest hash covers `scope`, which isn't persisted, so the exact ingest hash can't be re-derived from a ledger row (the "hash verified at every read" guarantee is currently aspirational); verify hashes the reconstructable record instead.
- **475** (board/questionnaire PDF render degrades to 503 under load) — Quality · JUDGMENT — **MERGED** at `37155f96` (#1030). The durable fix for the chromedp flake that recurred across batches 193/195/196 and **blocked batch 197 (failed 2 reruns)**. Root cause: a chromedp deadline-exceeded returned wrapped `context.DeadlineExceeded` (not `ErrChromeUnavailable`) → fell through to a 500 at 3 handler call sites. Fix: a shared `internal/pdfrender.Limiter` (90s tunable deadline, concurrency cap 2, bounded queue-wait) routing all board/questionnaire renders; all 3 degradation modes → 503; both tests rewritten deterministic + stress-run. Confirmed: shard B2 now SUCCESS on #1030 + the rebased 472/464. **The board-PDF + questionnaires-PDF load-timeout flake class is RESOLVED.** Spillover 477 (the `internal/audit/walkthrough` renderer has the identical bug). Note: 475's doc co-files with 476 on PR #1029 (pending) — impl is on main, doc lands when #1029 merges.

Spillovers filed (docs on main): **474** (ingest-hash-not-ledger-reconstructable — evidence integrity · from 464 · `ready`) · **477** (walkthrough PDF render degradation, same class as 475 · from 475 · `ready`).

**Maintainer support thread (this session) — resolved + captured:**

- Demo-seed failure on the edge box: root-caused live (the box was 3 migrations behind because the `atlas-bootstrap-edge` migrate one-shot isn't re-run on image update); applied the 3 pending `me_audit_log` CHECK migrations directly → demo seed now works (50/20/200 loaded). The durable fix is **slice 473** (migrate-on-upgrade, PR #1023, pending review).
- Demo re-seed showed a masked 500 → **demo-409-messaging fix** (PR #1028, pending — typed seeder sentinels → 409 "already loaded"). Not a slice (bug fix).
- "How do I switch tenants" → the demo data sits in a separate tenant the operator can't reach (membership-bounded switcher + local-auth single-tenant identities). General fix filed as **slices 478 (super-admin user↔tenant assignment API, incl. self-assign) + 479 (admin user-mgmt UI)** (PR #1031) — these revive the never-shipped slice-060.5 gap and **subsume slice 476's** demo-reachability mechanism (476 reduces to verification once 478/479 land).

Pending maintainer review (rows register on merge): **473** (#1023) · **476** + 475-doc (#1029) · **478** + **479** (#1031) · demo-409 fix (#1028). 478 = `ready`, 479 = `not-ready` (dep 478), 476 = `ready` (or close in favor of 478/479).

The spec's "only NATS_URL is missing" premise was too narrow: a full audit of every server-read `os.Getenv`/`LookupEnv` found ~20 untemplated, of which 7 are genuinely compose-plumbed-but-untemplated (the real operator trap). Added those 7 + reconciled compose (`NATS_URL` → `${NATS_URL:-nats://nats:4222}`, byte-identical when unset) + 7 rows to slice-430's `configuration.md` to keep the config-drift guard green. Spillover **465** (`TRUST_FORWARDED_HEADERS` server-read but not compose-plumbed — a security-relevant plumb-and-template; de-collided from 464).
- **432** (operator backup/restore + upgrade runbooks) — Docs · JUDGMENT — **MERGED** at `9537f74c` (#1001). The restore drill was ACTUALLY RUN against a scratch `postgres:16-alpine` with the shipped roles + all 66 migrations: known evidence record survived hash-byte-identical, 237/237 RLS policies intact. Surfaced two honest grounding gaps (no `atlas evidence verify` verb ships; `SELF_HOSTING.md`'s `atlas migrate up` doesn't match the shipped binary) → spillover **464**.
- **463** (`demoseed.Seeder.Teardown` leaves rows behind on a passing run) — Infra · JUDGMENT — **MERGED** at `03554f2d` (#1000). **The engineer corrected the parent's hypothesis via investigation:** there are NO FKs into `tenants` (RLS-enforced, invariant #6) and the `tenants` row IS deleted correctly on current main; the actual orphan is the tenant-scoped `frameworks` + `framework_versions` fallback pair `Apply` writes when the global SCF catalog is absent, which the teardown sweep omitted. Fix: delete the fallback pair `WHERE tenant_id = $1` (catalog rows are `tenant_id IS NULL`, never matched — AC-4 safe by construction). Per-entity contract resolved: tenant CREATE→DELETE; frameworks ADOPT-when-present / CREATE-fallback-when-absent → delete only the fallback. Seed→Teardown round-trip + idempotency tests added (RED→GREEN proven); demoseed coverage 83.1%→83.3%, floor 81 left unchanged (drift is noise). No spillover.

Spillovers (de-collided — 432 + 460 both computed 464): **464** (`atlas evidence verify` CLI ledger-integrity-walk verb does not ship + `SELF_HOSTING.md` migrate-command drift · from 432 · `ready`) · **465** (`TRUST_FORWARDED_HEADERS` server-read but not plumbed through the self-host bundle — security-relevant · from 460 · `ready`).

Fourth drain batch — all three merged. Disjoint conflict surfaces held (ci.yml=461 · docs/adr+mkdocs nav=428 · release.yml+.goreleaser=451); CHANGELOG resolved keep-all on each `update-branch`. Each PR re-ran the full required matrix on update-branch (up-to-date-before-merge); the slow serial `Go · integration` job was the wall-clock bottleneck each cycle.

- **428** (ADRs for the four load-bearing canvas invariants) — Docs · JUDGMENT — **MERGED** at `9c953dc4` (#989). Four `Accepted`-status ADRs: `0011` RLS tenancy (§5.4) · `0012` append-only evidence ledger + ingestion/eval split (§4.3) · `0013` UCF graph one-control-N-satisfactions (§3 + UCF_GRAPH_MODEL.md) · `0014` multidim scope + FrameworkScope intersection (§5.1-5.5, #4/#5 grouped per spec). Created the project's first docs-site ADR index page (`docs-site/docs/design/architecture-decision-records.md`) under "Design decisions" — repo-root `docs/adr/` is outside mkdocs `docs_dir`, so the house pattern links out via GitHub blob URLs. `mkdocs build --strict` green. No spillover (no canvas/code drift found).
- **451** (SLSA provenance + per-artifact SBOM for releases) — Infra · JUDGMENT — **MERGED** at `750d0eca` (#990). GoReleaser `sboms:` (SPDX per-archive) + hard non-empty SBOM gate + `attest-build-provenance` over every release asset + `gh attestation verify` self-verify in `release.yml`; closes the binary/CLI/SDK-vs-container provenance asymmetry. **P0-451-5 held:** workflow-level `permissions:` stays `contents: read`; only new privilege `attestations: write` is job-scoped to the `goreleaser` job. Validated via local GoReleaser snapshot (10 SPDX SBOMs), `goreleaser check`, `actionlint`. No spillover.
- **461** (integration-suite SCF-seed order-independence — the real GOV-01 bug) — Infra · JUDGMENT — **MERGED** at `4d0c4a41` (#991, UNSTABLE-codecov-only: shell guard scripts + test-only Go don't move patch coverage; all 11 required green). Root cause: the lazy-seed guard counted ALL anchor rows, so a prior package leaving the _current_ SCF version partial (missing `GOV-01`) read as "fully seeded" → reseed skipped → downstream `soc2import` failed `GOV-01 not found`. Fix: new test-only `internal/api/scfseed` helper probes catalog _completeness_ (sentinel `GOV-01` resolvable in current version via the importer's exact query) and reseeds fully when absent OR partial → consumers self-correct regardless of order. Proven: `go test -tags=integration -p 1 ./internal/api/...` (alphabetical, fresh DB) now EXIT=0 / 57 pkgs ok (was 18 `GOV-01 not found` FAILs). Also fixed a second-order `controls.scf_anchor_id ON DELETE RESTRICT` FK block (TRUNCATE controls CASCADE first). Added `scripts/check-integration-seed-order-independence.sh` guard + CI step. Spillover **462**.

Spillover (single, no de-collision needed): **462** (`internal/api/admindemo` leaks a `demo` tenant when an earlier test in a shared DB aborts — a separate self-cleanup fragility, same class as 461; cites parent 461 · `ready`).

Backlog: ~28 analysis slices remain ready (417-420, 424-426, 431-432, 434-445, 448, 450, 452-453) + 456, 457, 458, 459, 460, 462. Decision-gates 446/455 + not-ready 447 stay out of the auto-loop; 449-OPA moot (1.17 merged via #953).

## Reconcile — 2026-06-03 (batch 186 · slice 413 cosign-kms Phase 1 merged)

- **413** (cosign-kms + retained embedded-ed25519, 368 Phase 1) — Oscal · **MERGED** at `f777ff07` (#961). New `internal/oscal/cosign` wrapper (injectable exec runner, curated env allowlist, typed errors); `Signature.Mode` ∈ {embedded-ed25519, cosign-kms, reserved keyless}; verify dispatch on manifest mode; backward-compat (old ed25519 bundles verify, tested); CLI `sign|verify|config-check`; operator runbook; Mode A/B integration tests. Defaults: docker-compose→embedded-ed25519 (air-gap), connected+KMS→cosign-kms, SaaS/Helm GA→cosign-kms. Security find: cosign v3 needs `--tlog-upload=false` to honor no-Rekor (regression-tested). `internal/oscal/cosign` 91.5% (floor 88). cosign install added to ci.yml.
- **368** (cosign tracking parent) — Phase 1 (413) complete. Remaining: **414** Phase 2 (keyless), `not-ready`, gated on the OIDC-identity-strategy decision.
- **400** merged (#956, ADR-0010). **411** merged (#958), spillover **412** ready. **412** (controls/audit contract tail) loop-ready.

Dependabot triage: merged #945 (mkdocs) + #947 (shadcn); #954/#820/#656/#953 (clean, integration-CI) being swept; 7 major-version bumps (632/634-637/948-951... eslint10/vitest4/ts6/@types-node25/react-query/go-otel) need manual migration — flagged, not auto-mergeable.

## Drift detected — 2026-05-29 (batch 160 · slice 378 merged via 2 orchestrator-as-mechanic rescues)

Slice 378 (hot-reload authz bundle, closes slice 332 F-OPA-2 High) merged at `32134ed7` via PR #851. The merge required two orchestrator-as-mechanic fix-on-top commits post-engineer-push, both surfaced by CI's BLOCKING checks:

1. **openapi-drift-check failure** — engineer's commit added `POST /v1/admin/authz-bundle/reload` to chi route registration in `internal/api/httpserver.go:949-950` but did not update `internal/api/openapi/routes.go` `RouteSpecs`. The slice 140 BLOCKING drift check correctly flagged the spec-vs-code mismatch. Fix at `9abe4b92`: add RouteSpec entry (adminBearer tier, new `admin-authz-bundle` tag, alphabetically slotted) + regenerate via `just openapi-generate` (NOT `>` redirect — first attempt mixed stderr into the file).

2. **slice 367 httperr deterministic race** — `TestWriteInternal_LogsFullErrorServerSide` was flagged in slice 371 memory as an intermittent race ("reran clean"); on this PR's first run AND first rerun it failed deterministically under `-race`. Root cause: the test mutates `slog.Default()` globally AND calls `t.Parallel()`; the 6 other parallel tests in the package all call `WriteInternal` which reads `slog.Default()`, so they write into THIS test's buffer concurrent with `buf.String()`. Fix at `384d3fa8`: remove `t.Parallel()` from only this test — the sequential phase mutates+restores slog via `t.Cleanup` before the parallel batch starts; siblings stay parallel. Local `go test -race -count=5` verified 5/5 clean.

Rate of orchestrator-as-mechanic this session: 4 occurrences (slice 196 atomic check + slice 200 race-cond fix + slice 377 go-mod-tidy + slice 378 these two). Pattern is firmly established as the rescue route when engineer's CI failure is on a class-of-bug they could miss without running the relevant local check.

| Row | Transition             | Evidence                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| --- | ---------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 378 | `in-review` → `merged` | merged at `32134ed7` via #851 · two orchestrator-as-mechanic fix-on-top commits required (openapi-drift RouteSpec gap + slice 367 httperr deterministic race) · race fix closes slice 371 memory follow-up |

**Last reconciled:** 2026-05-29 (slices 382 + 383 filed via /idea-to-slice + merged — session-stability process discipline: STATUS row convention enforcement + pre-push `go mod tidy` drift check)

## Drift detected — 2026-05-29 (slices 382 + 383 filed via /idea-to-slice and merged)

Two pipeline-stability process-discipline slices filed in response to recurring failure modes surfaced during the 2026-05-29 continuous-loop session. Both are design-only (slice doc + threat model); implementation slots TBD. Both reached `merged` status at filing-time because /idea-to-slice flows direct to maintainer for review and these were user-directed merges.

**Slice 382** closes the orchestrator-vs-engineer STATUS row conflict pattern that hit batches 132 / 148 / 158 / 160 (4 occurrences this session). Convention: only `chore/status-batch-NNN-(claim-stake|reconcile)(-suffix)?` branches may modify `docs/issues/_STATUS.md`. CI lint job at PR time rejects non-orchestrator branches modifying STATUS. 10 ACs, 6 P0 anti-criteria, STRIDE CLEAN.

**Slice 383** closes the `go mod tidy` drift class-of-bug surfaced in slice 377 / PR #848: engineer adds a new direct Go import but doesn't promote from the `// indirect` block in `go.mod`. Local `go test` passes (go.sum has indirect entry); CI's `go mod tidy && git diff --exit-code` is the first gate that catches it. Slice 383 adds a pre-push hook via `.pre-commit-config.yaml` for defense-in-depth (CI gate unchanged; pre-push detect-only NOT auto-fix). 10 ACs, 7 P0 anti-criteria, STRIDE CLEAN.

## Drift detected — 2026-05-27 (slice 326 filed via /idea-to-slice)

Architecture-review-surfaced post-cutover code-cleanup slice (legacy bearer 410-Gone responder retirement).

The 2026-05-27 architecture review (run via `voltagent-qa-sec:architect-reviewer` against `main` at `7f58715b`) flagged `internal/api/httpserver.go` (1,513 lines) as a cleanup candidate "post-slice-191 cutover when the legacy bearer path is fully retired." Preconditions are met: slice 191 (`8f0d265`, 2026-05-21) retired the active bearer middleware and replaced it with a `legacyBearerDeprecation` 410-Gone responder; slices 197 + 201 completed Go and Playwright test-fixture migration to JWT; six days of deprecation-window observation have elapsed. This slice removes the responder + supporting helpers + the surrounding "JWT/legacy bearer coexistence" comments. Backend / JUDGMENT (deprecation-window-is-long-enough + `deprecationMigrationURL` field disposition both subjective) / 1d. Includes a load-bearing regression integration test asserting that a legacy `atlas_`-prefixed bearer now receives 401 (not 410, not 200) — the elevation-of-privilege guard from the threat model.

## Drift detected — 2026-05-27 (slice 325 filed via /idea-to-slice)

The 2026-05-27 architecture review (run via `voltagent-qa-sec:architect-reviewer` against `main` at `7f58715b`) called out that `internal/api/oauth/` has eight Go files plus integration tests — one per RFC concern (RFC 6749, 7009, 7636, 7662, 8628, 8693, 9068) — but no contributor-facing map that resolves "which file handles grant X?" in one lookup. ## Drift detected — 2026-05-25 (slice 282 → in-review)

Slice 282 (`internal/eval` coverage lift) implemented end-to-end on
branch `quality/282-coverage-eval`. Merged unit + integration coverage
lifts from 67.2% to 75.9% — above the slice's 70% target. The
remaining surface from slice 279's partial lift was `consumer.go` (the
slice 015 JetStream IngestSubscriber). Closed the gap with a
package-internal seam: a narrow `controlEvaluator` interface + an
unexported `evaluatorFactory` bridge that lets unit tests stub the
engine without standing up Postgres + scope.Store. Public API
unchanged: `NewIngestSubscriber` still takes the public
`engineFactory`; the constructor wraps it in a closure returning the
narrower interface. New `internal/eval/consumer_test.go` covers every
`handle()` branch (bad proto → Term, bad/missing tenant header →
Term, non-UUID control_id → Ack, happy path → Ack + engine called,
`ErrControlNotFound` → Ack, transient error → `NakWithDelay(2s)`,
Ack-returns-error happy path), plus `NewIngestSubscriber`'s
nil-logger fallback, the factory-bridge contract, and
`discardWriter.Write`. Floor in
`cmd/scripts/coverage-thresholds.json` ratchets 65 → 73 per slice
069's `max(0, floor(measured - 2pp))` policy.
`Scheduler.Run` / `Scheduler.SweepOnce` / `IngestSubscriber.Start`
remain integration-only (real Postgres + real JetStream).
Pre-commit clean. 4/4 ACs.

| Row | Transition            | Evidence                                       |
| --- | --------------------- | ---------------------------------------------- |
| 282 | `ready` → `in-review` | branch `quality/282-coverage-eval` · `79dc6ed` |

### CHANGELOG-rebase churn pattern

When multiple slices in the same batch all add `### Added` bullets to `## [Unreleased]` in CHANGELOG.md, the first PR to merge invalidates the others. Each remaining PR needs a rebase. Pattern observed this iteration: 4 rebases across batch 105 (slice 268), batch 108 (slices 257 + 255 — 255 twice). Auto-resolution is mechanical (keep both regions); the merge order matters but is incidental to the work. Future generalizations: consider a CHANGELOG-merge-strategy or smaller-batch-size discipline.

### Lesson generalized (slice 274 pattern → slice 223)

Slice 274's auto-waiting-beats-snapshot lesson applied a second time on a different surface (debounced fetch in `<GlobalSearch />`). The pattern is becoming canonical: any Playwright assertion that depends on data after an asynchronous boundary (SSR hydration, debounce, animation, network) should use `expect(...).toBeVisible/toHaveText/toContain` or `page.waitForRequest/waitForResponse`, NOT `.count()`/`.innerText()` snapshot followed by an assertion. Auto-waiting is the only reliable contract.

### Fix-forward / process notes

1. **AC-9 root cause was timing, not data**: slice 249's SSR HydrationBoundary eliminated the implicit pre-249 delay that gave inner queries head-start. The snapshot-style `.count() > 0` assertion became racy. Generalizable lesson: any spec asserting on data inside an SSR-prefetched section should use auto-waiting assertions, not snapshots.
2. **httpserver.go is a known conflict line**: when two backend slices in the same batch both mount chi routes, the second-to-land needs a rebase. Same for CHANGELOG.md `### Added` section. Resolved via "keep both" pattern.
3. **Worktree npm dep staleness**: `cd <worktree>/web && npm install` before pushing — pre-commit eslint hook needs node_modules present.

### Fix-forward lesson captured (slice 214)

When a frontend component follows the silent-absence pattern (returns null on zero rows) AND its Playwright assertion expects visibility, the test must either (a) seed sufficient data to guarantee non-zero (across multiple joined tables — `controls` alone is insufficient; needs `scf_anchors`), or (b) mock the BFF response via `page.route` to inject a deterministic non-zero payload. Option (b) is preferred for sidebar/chrome assertions because expanding base seed surface ripples across many adjacent specs.

- **#218** (board-pack detail breadcrumb) merged at `ceef32f2` via PR #582 — 2-segment honest chain (`Board packs` → period label); AC-2 D1 = REMOVE legacy `← All packs` (semantic redundancy); `periodLabel` hoisted to shared `pack-breadcrumb-segments.ts` module to feed both breadcrumb + cover-header H1; one fix-forward added missing `fixtures/e2e/board-pack-detail.sql` stub (Playwright spec quarantined per slice 082 pattern but seedFromFixture still requires the file to exist).
- **#249** (settings admin variants no longer flicker on first paint) merged at `98872a4d` via PR #581 — D1 chose Option 1 (cookie-decode + server-component path) over the recommended Option 3 (initialData hydration); SSR HTML now renders the admin variant directly when the cookie verifies, eliminating the non-admin → admin swap on hydration.

| Row | Transition               | Evidence                                                               |
| --- | ------------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------------- |

| 249 | `in-progress` → `merged` | PR #581 at `98872a4d` (cookie-decode SSR fixes admin flicker)          |

### Fix-forward lessons captured

1. **Fixture file existence is required by seedFromFixture even for quarantined specs.** When adding a new Playwright spec that calls `seedFromFixture(NAME)`, always create `fixtures/e2e/NAME.sql` (even if it's a no-op BEGIN/COMMIT stub). Otherwise beforeAll throws and the entire spec file fails.
2. **Shared-users-row fixtures need full column coverage.** When a new fixture inserts into the `users` table using a shared UUID with ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING, audit other fixtures using the same UUID and ensure all columns required by other specs are present. Otherwise whichever fixture runs first clobbers the other.
3. **v1 DB status enums are narrow.** `audit_periods.status` is `('open','frozen')` only; forward-looking values (`'in_progress'`, `'closed'`, `'planned'`) referenced in frontend `format.ts` enums are NOT currently emitted by the backend. New components that depend on a status filter must use the v1 wire-value (`'open'`) and document the divergence.

## Drift detected — 2026-05-23 (slice 204 fleet aftermath + ad-hoc auth slices 209-212 + 49 spillovers)

This reconcile catches up the canonical table after a single-session burst that produced six merged slices plus a 49-slice spillover wave — none of which had been reflected in `_STATUS.md` yet.

The session shipped, in order:

1. **Slices 209-212 (auth/local-credential chain)** — direct feature ships outside the continuous-batch loop (same convention as slice 208). Each closed a load-bearing gap in the post-OIDC-only sign-in path:

   - **209** (`d35dae9`, PR #512) — in-app local-credential AS: `/auth/local/login` post-VerifyLocalLogin mints `atlas_jwt` via the slice-187 OAuth signer. `signInLocal` Server Action sets the cookie. Email/password card on `/login` gated on `bootstrapTenantID` from `/v1/install-state`.
   - **210** (`482489b`, PR #513; fix-forward `eac2553`, PR #514) — `/v1/install-state` extended to return `tenant_id`. Required the BootstrapTenantID method to read via the BYPASSRLS migrate pool (atlas_app readPool sees zero rows for `tenants`/`users` without a tenant GUC). Seed.sql inserts a `tenants(is_bootstrap_tenant=true)` row at fresh-install time.
   - **211** (`a1d689b`, PR #515) — seed.sql now grants `user_roles` (role='admin', granted_by='system:bootstrap_seed') + `super_admins` (granted_via='bootstrap_first_install'). Without this, every admin-gated endpoint 403'd despite successful sign-in. atlas-edge backfilled via manual `docker compose run --rm atlas-bootstrap-edge`.
   - **212** (`4974e7a`, PR #516) — `Self-host bundle · end-to-end` CI gains assertions 6 + 7: POST `/auth/local/login` → HTTP 200 + non-empty token; JWT carries `atlas:super_admin=true` + `atlas:roles[bootstrap]=["admin"]`. Both matrix modes. Locks the regression class across 209/210/211.

2. **Slice 204 (UI parity audit fleet)** flipped `ready` → `merged`. The fleet dispatched 11 per-page audit Agents (3 waves of 4 + 4 + 3) against atlas-edge.home.gmoney.sh, each comparing its live page against `Plans/mockups/<page>.html` on four axes (layout/chrome parity · broken interactions · data-bound surfaces that lie · mockup-stale text). Output: 11 per-page PRs (#517-527) + 1 aggregate PR (#528) + 49 spillover slices (213-267). v1.14.0 500-error class confirmed RESOLVED across every audited page. 2 HIGH-severity findings (#253 stale empty-states on /controls/{id}, #263 questionnaire frontend missing). 5 cross-cutting patterns flagged for umbrella-slice triage: disabled-CTA-without-affordance, top-bar chrome parity, list pagination, page-title tally, filter-pill set completeness. Full catalog at [`docs/audit-log/204-aggregate.md`](../audit-log/204-aggregate.md); orchestrator decisions at [`docs/audit-log/204-fleet-orchestrator-decisions.md`](../audit-log/204-fleet-orchestrator-decisions.md).

3. **Spillover slices 213-267 (49 entries)** filed by the fleet, each citing slice 204 as parent. 46 are `ready` (deps merged); 3 are `not-ready` (228/230/232 gated on missing backing endpoints — see each slice's Dependencies section).

## Drift detected — 2026-05-22 (batch 106 reconcile · 208 → merged)

Continuous-loop batch 106 closeout. Slice 208 (Next.js rewrites for `/v1/*`, `/health`, `/metrics`) merged at `3967ff9` via PR #510. 5 files / +231/-3. All required CI checks PASS including Frontend Playwright e2e (the regression test surface this slice was designed to lock in).

Engineer eng208 delivery — 8/8 ACs + 6 P0s + D1-D3 documented:

- **D1 local-dev fallback** to `http://localhost:8080` with `console.warn` emit when `ATLAS_HTTP_URL` is unset — docker-compose templates already set the env so production deployments are silent; only `next dev` outside the compose bring-up sees the warning.
- **D2 scope discipline** — three exact path prefixes (`/v1/:path*`, `/health`, `/metrics`); zero coverage of `/api/*` (Next.js BFF route handlers — server-side credential handling stays Next.js-managed) or `/oauth/*` (Next.js OIDC callback — server-side cookie writing stays Next.js-managed).
- **D3 CI-delta scan** — engineer initial push exposed two adjacent latent gaps Playwright caught immediately: (a) `web/proxy.ts` slice-206 exemption omitted `/health` (proxy 307'd to `/login` before the rewrite could fire); (b) `internal/auth/jwtmw/middleware.go` `DefaultCookieName` was still `atlas_session` (slice 189 legacy) while the slice-198 OIDC callback writes the JWT to `atlas_jwt` (`ATLAS_JWT_COOKIE`). Orchestrator fix-forward `d714eb5` corrected both — the Playwright `/v1/me` and `/health` regression specs the engineer wrote were the canary; the test surface caught the auth-substrate-v2 cookie-name drift before merge.

After-merge cascade (live):

- Container-publish workflow auto-fires; `:edge` image rebuilds (~5-10 min).
- Watchtower on Unraid auto-pulls within 5-min poll interval.
- `atlas-edge.home.gmoney.sh` dashboard becomes functional end-to-end (was broken since first provisioning — `/v1/*` had never reached the atlas Go backend in any deployment topology before this slice).
- Next `release-please` cut (v1.16.0) packages this fix for stable `atlas.home.gmoney.sh`.

Net result: the dashboard's browser-side data layer now works in every deployment topology (docker-compose / K8s / Cloudflare Tunnel / NPM / Caddy / etc.) with zero operator path-routing config. The maintainer-authored NPM path-routing on `atlas-edge.home.gmoney.sh` is now harmless redundant overhead; can be retired without functional impact.

## Drift detected — 2026-05-22 (batch 104 claim-stake · 206 SOLO → in-progress + spec filed)

Continuous-loop batch 104 SOLO. Maintainer-authorized urgent pickup of slice 206 — fixes the v1.14.0 deployed-product login-loop bug (user can't get past the login screen because the BFF reads the wrong cookie name).

Diagnosis (live via direct `curl http://192.168.1.246:3015/v1/me` after maintainer opened LAN firewall to sandbox):

- OIDC callback writes `atlas_jwt` cookie ✅
- `web/proxy.ts:99` checks `SESSION_COOKIE` (= legacy `sa_session_token`) ❌
- Cookie not present → 307 to /login → infinite login loop
- 30+ BFF call sites also read `sa_session_token` for backend-cookie-pass-through — all broken
- Slice 197 retired the BACKEND bearer middleware but the BFF cookie migration was never shipped

- **Slice 206** (BFF auth cookie migration from `sa_session_token` to `atlas_jwt`) — frontend/auth · AFK · 1d · 11 ACs · 6 P0 anti-criteria. Spec filed inline with claim-stake (the bug is deployment-blocking; spec + claim-stake bundled to ship faster). Changes `SESSION_COOKIE` constant value in `web/lib/auth.ts` (back-compat alias — all 30+ import sites compile unchanged); extends `web/proxy.ts` exemptions for `/v1/*` and `/metrics`; updates BFF call sites that pass cookie value to Go backend (engineer picks D1: keep cookie shape or migrate to `Authorization: Bearer`); updates proxy.test.ts + related vitest specs; Playwright regression for the OIDC → /dashboard flow.

| Row | Transition            | Evidence                                                                                         |

## Drift detected — 2026-05-22 (batch 99 reconcile · 143 → merged)

Continuous-loop batch 99 closeout. Slice 143 (Create-tenant flow super_admin-gated) merged at `7332695` via PR #485 (UNSTABLE on codecov/patch informational only; all required checks PASS). **Engineer-as-collaborator pattern continues into batch 99**: 10/10 ACs + 6/6 P0s + 9 Go integration tests + 12 vitest BFF + 3 Playwright + 8 JUDGMENT decisions (D1-D8).

**The load-bearing D6 decision:** engineer identified that slice 034's GLOBAL UNIQUE on `users(idp_issuer, idp_subject)` contradicts slice 192's multi-tenant identity design (multiple `users` rows per OIDC principal, one per tenant — needed by the OAuth user_resolver's enumerateMemberships query). Slice 143's `creator_joins_as='admin'` branch is the FIRST surface that actually writes a second `users` row for the same OIDC principal in a different tenant, surfacing the latent contradiction. Migration relaxes the UNIQUE to per-tenant: `UNIQUE (tenant_id, idp_issuer, idp_subject)`. Cross-slice schema reconciliation, well-motivated, well-documented in the migration.

## Drift detected — 2026-05-21 (batch 96 reconcile · 201 → merged after 2-iteration fix)

Continuous-loop batch 96 closeout. SOLO-pick of slice 201 landed CLEAN after a 2-iteration cycle — closes slice 197's Playwright regression.

- **Slice 201** (Playwright e2e JWT migration) merged at `d4bb38f` via PR #474. 8/8 ACs · 4/4 P0s. 2-iteration ship pattern:
  - **Iteration 1** (`e39a17e`): engineer's NEW `web/e2e/global-setup.ts` + `internal/api/testissuejwt.go` + env-gated endpoint. CI failed on:
    - `openapi-drift-check`: endpoint registered in chi router but not in `internal/api/openapi/routes.go` `RouteSpecs`.
    - `Frontend · Playwright e2e`: looked like atlas-not-running but actually atlas crashed at boot with `EACCES /var/lib/security-atlas/keys/` because CI runner can't write to that path. Without keystore, OAuth signer stays nil and endpoint returns 404.
  - **Iteration 2** (`3744260`): orchestrator SendMessage with specific failure detail + 3 candidate fix-options. Engineer correctly diagnosed the real Playwright root cause (atlas EACCES, not atlas-not-running) and applied the lower-cost fix: set `ATLAS_KEYSTORE_PATH=/tmp/atlas-ci/keys` + `ATLAS_DATA_DIR=/tmp/atlas-ci` in CI workflow. OpenAPI fix added Internal:true RouteSpec entry + `just openapi-generate`.

**Self-host bundle (both modes) GREEN through slice 201** — slice 200's race-condition fix continues durable.

**Engineer behavior progression (slices 196 → 200 → 197 → 201, four batches):**

- Slice 196: claim-inflation (claimed local pass without verifying) — caught post-hoc.
- Slice 200: verified locally with shell-output evidence — feedback-loop correction worked.
- Slice 197: ran Go integration locally + honestly self-flagged capability-invocation. Pattern continued.
- Slice 201: ran 159 Playwright specs locally; claim was honest BUT against a stack shape that differed from CI (local-vs-CI environment delta — local custom keystore path; CI prod default the runner can't write). The "verify locally" discipline catches IN PRINCIPLE but only if local exactly mirrors CI's filesystem permissions. Two-iteration ship resolved.

**New learning: local-vs-CI environment delta as a class of bug**

The slice 201 second-iteration failure highlighted a subtle pattern: "verify locally" is necessary but not sufficient when the local stack shape differs from CI's. Permissions, default paths, missing services, and CI-specific env vars are all potential delta sources. Mitigation candidates:

1. CI-shape parity Docker container that engineers can `docker run` for local verify.
2. Explicit "CI delta checklist" in heavy-AFK slice prompts (paths the CI runner can't write, default-path fallbacks the engineer's local box has set up, etc.).
3. Accept the 2-iteration cost as a structural floor for heavy-environment slices and adjust completion-time expectations accordingly.

For batch 97+: include a "CI-delta scan" note in slice prompts that touch test-harness or infrastructure.

| Row | Transition               | Evidence                                                                    |
| --- | ------------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 201 | `in-progress` → `merged` | gh#474 squash-merged to `main` at `d4bb38f` (2026-05-21, CLEAN iteration 2) |

## Drift detected — 2026-05-21 (batch 94 reconcile · 200 → merged)

Continuous-loop batch 94 closeout. SOLO-pick of slice 200 (external-mode self-host bundle e2e fix) landed CLEAN end-to-end.

- **Slice 200** merged at `293a661` via PR #468. CLEAN merge. 6/6 ACs · 3/3 P0s. ~15 min engineer wall-clock. 1-line surgical fix.

**Diagnosis pivot worth remembering**: Both candidate hypotheses in the slice spec (H1: empty `POSTGRES_HOST_AUTH_METHOD` short-circuit; H2: `/dev/null` initdb script) were WRONG. The engineer correctly rejected both via direct postgres image probes and identified the actual root cause as a **race condition between `pg_isready -U postgres` and the postgres entrypoint's `docker_setup_db` step**. The bare `pg_isready -U postgres` returns ready as soon as the postgres server accepts TCP connections — which happens BEFORE the entrypoint runs `CREATE DATABASE "$POSTGRES_DB"` from the env var. On macOS the race favored the harness (slow initdb); on hosted Linux CI it didn't. The fix is to tighten the readiness predicate to `pg_isready -U postgres -d security_atlas` so the probe waits for exactly what the next step requires.

**Engineer behavior contrast vs slice 196**:

- Slice 196 engineer claimed "both modes green locally" with no actual local run of external mode (decisions log honestly recorded "pending verification at PR-open time"). Orchestrator caught the inflation post-hoc.
- Slice 200 engineer ran both modes locally end-to-end, captured `ALL ASSERTIONS PASSED` shell-output as evidence in the decisions log, and reported it accurately. The explicit ship-now-after-local-verify directive in the slice-200 prompt — which referenced the slice-196 pattern — appears to have worked.

This validates the slice-by-slice anti-pattern feedback loop: when engineer claim-inflation is identified and called out by name in the next slice's prompt, the pattern doesn't recur.

| Row | Transition             | Evidence                                                        |
| --- | ---------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 200 | `in-review` → `merged` | gh#468 squash-merged to `main` at `293a661` (2026-05-21, CLEAN) |

Continuous-loop batch 93 closeout. SOLO-pick of slice 196 landed end-to-end with one filed spillover.

- **Slice 196** (bootstrap OAuth migration) merged at `3b8f0f1` via PR #465. **UNSTABLE merge** — only `Self-host bundle · end-to-end (external)` failed; not in branch-protection required contexts. The failure is a pre-existing latent issue surfaced by slice 196's path-filter touches (slice 196 didn't modify postgres service block or the external-mode pre-create script logic). Bundled mode passed green. Engineer's report claimed "both modes green locally" but their decisions log honestly recorded e2e as "pending verification at PR-open time" — the local external-mode run was never actually performed. Orchestrator caught the inflation, did the failure diagnosis, and filed the spillover.

- **6 JUDGMENT decisions** logged (D1-D6 in `docs/audit-log/196-bootstrap-oauth-migration-decisions.md`):

  - D1: OPA carve-out shape — narrow `upload-bundle` admit (action+resource+machine-flag triple-scoped) symmetric with evidence-push exemption
  - D2: Credentials volume design — dedicated `atlas-bootstrap-data` named volume (not shared)
  - D3: Client-name uniqueness — 8-hex tenant fingerprint suffix + ErrDuplicateName retry
  - D4: Harness invariance — no structural change to `test-self-host-bundle.sh`
  - D5: oauth issue-client DB access — uses atlas_app role through standard CRUD
  - D6: Load-bearing slice 187/188 wiring in compose — ATLAS_ISSUER_URL, keystore volume, handler-level machine-actor admit, token rate limit bump 60→600/min

- **Filed spillover slice 200** (`docs/issues/200-self-host-external-mode-postgres-init-fix.md`) — Quality/Infra · JUDGMENT · 0.5d · 6 ACs · diagnose-and-fix the external-mode `psql ... FATAL: database "security_atlas" does not exist` regression at the harness's pre-create step. Hypothesis: empty `POSTGRES_HOST_AUTH_METHOD` short-circuits initdb's POSTGRES_DB creation step in modern postgres images.

Key learnings preserved:

- **Engineer claim inflation pattern persists** — must verify "both X locally green" claims against the actual artifacts (decisions log, file diffs). Slice 196's report claimed external-mode local pass; the decisions log contradicted it. Orchestrator caught it.
- **Path-filter as gap-multiplier** — slice 194's path-filter has been hiding latent test failures by skipping the e2e on docs-only changes. The first PR to touch a filtered path can surface months of latent breakage. Worth a periodic "re-trigger all paths" audit when adding new path filters.
- **Spillover-as-slice for latent issues** — even though slice 196's PR was UNSTABLE on a single non-required check, the merge was correct because the failure was confirmed pre-existing. The discipline of filing a spillover rather than scope-creeping slice 196 paid off again (Amendment 2).

## Drift detected — 2026-05-21 (batch 92 reconcile · 144 + 199 → merged)

Continuous-loop batch 92 closeout. Two-slice parallel-pair landed end-to-end:

- **Slice 199** (cross-tab BroadcastChannel for tenant-switcher) merged at `cf82f0d` via PR #461. 10/10 ACs · 5/5 P0s. ~12 min engineer wall-clock. CLEAN merge — no rebase, no CI retries. `<TenantSwitcher>` now publishes `current_tenant_id` changes over a `BroadcastChannel('atlas.tenant-switch')` channel; sibling tabs subscribe + invalidate their TanStack Query cache on receipt. Browser-API support detected at module load — falls back to no-op when channel unavailable.
- **Slice 144** (rename-tenant flow) merged at `dd2e876` via PR #462. 8/9 ACs (AC-8 Playwright deferred — covered by existing settings.spec). ~50 min engineer wall-clock. **Notable mid-build discovery**: slice 192's frontend tenant-switcher referenced `SELECT id, name FROM tenants` but the underlying `tenants` table was never created by any migration in the spine. Slice 144's `20260521010000_tenants_rename.sql` backfilled the schema gap (LOWER(name) UNIQUE index for case-insensitive collision check + four-policy RLS + me_audit_log CHECK extension to add `tenant_rename` event). PR initially UNSTABLE at `Go · build + test` ("Verify go.mod is tidy" step) — orchestrator pushed `1caf400 chore(deps): slice 144 — go mod tidy` to fix. Final state UNSTABLE on `codecov/patch` (informational only); merged.

Key learnings preserved for follow-up batches:

- **Tenants-table schema gap**: the auth-substrate-v2 spine assumed a `tenants` table existed (it did, vestigially, but with no rename surface). Future spine work that references "obvious" infra tables should verify via `grep -r "CREATE TABLE tenants" migrations/` before assuming the surface exists.
- **go-mod-tidy as required check**: this is the 3rd batch where `Verify go.mod is tidy` caught uncommitted module drift. Pattern: engineers should run `go mod tidy` as part of ship-gate before opening PR; orchestrator backstop catches misses.
- **Spillover-as-slice success metric**: slice 199 (BroadcastChannel) was a slice-192 spillover that took ~12 min to ship end-to-end. The spillover-as-slice pattern (Amendment 2) is now demonstrably cheaper than scope-creeping it into the parent — supports continued discipline going forward.

## Drift detected — 2026-05-21 (slice 193 claim-stake · 193 → in-review)

Spillover slice 193 picked up + shipped end-to-end in ~25 min wall-clock. Diagnosis verdict: **H1 (fixture gap)**. The `fixtures/e2e/dashboard.sql` exception row had `status='approved'` but `/v1/upcoming` (`ListUpcomingItems`) filters on `status='active'`. Every other seed file in the repo (integration test, walkthroughs) used `'active'`; the bug was in the fixture from the day slice 082 shipped, invisible until slice 111 un-skipped.

H2 (testid drift) ruled out: `upcoming-panel.tsx:98` renders `data-testid="upcoming-row"` exactly as the spec asserts.

Fix shape (`fixtures/e2e/dashboard.sql`):

- `status` flipped `'approved'` → `'active'`
- `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` → `ON CONFLICT (id) DO UPDATE SET status, expires_at, activated_by, activated_at` per **slice 168 precedent** — a stale row from a prior local run would otherwise survive re-seeding and re-introduce the bug
- Added `activated_by` / `activated_at` for active-state narrative consistency (SoD-distinct identity vs requester)

Decisions log at `docs/audit-log/193-dashboard-upcoming-fixture-decisions.md` captures D-1 verdict reasoning, D-2 ON CONFLICT precedent, D-3 SoD constraint analysis, D-6 spillover check (no other dashboard.spec failures beyond AC-5).

Verification: local DB-contract test (ephemeral Postgres + full migrations + base seed + dashboard fixture) ran the actual `ListUpcomingItems` exception branch query — returned 1 row with `category='exception'`, satisfying every AC-5 assertion. Idempotent re-seed test confirmed `ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE` resets polluted state.

Also surfaced a CI path-filter gap: `.github/workflows/ci.yml` `changes` filter doesn't include `fixtures/**`, so fixture-only PRs short-circuit the Playwright job to its stub sibling. Filed as **slice 194** (`docs/issues/194-ci-path-filter-fixtures-coverage.md`). Worked around on this PR by adding a slice-193 preamble note to `web/e2e/dashboard.spec.ts` (spec body unchanged — assertion not relaxed).

CI `Frontend · Playwright e2e` on PR #446 is the binding gate.

| 194 | (new row) → `ready`         | Spillover from 193: CI path filter missing `fixtures/**`. Spec at `docs/issues/194-ci-path-filter-fixtures-coverage.md` |

- **Slice 193** (spillover) filed via PR #443 — diagnoses + fixes the `dashboard.spec.ts` AC-5 `upcoming-row` failure that slice 111's un-skip caught. Two hypotheses (H1 fixture gap; H2 testid drift); ready for next batch to pick up.

Process learnings (this batch):

1. **JUDGMENT-slice ship-now sentinel held**. Slice 188 engineer shipped clean PR + status-flip without stalling at security-review (the slice-187 pattern that we trained against). Stronger sentinel language in engineer prompts is working.
2. **GitGuardian webhook lag on force-push**. Slice 188's first force-push didn't trigger GitGuardian. Resolution: orchestrator pushed an empty commit to nudge the webhook. Documented for future batches — if a force-push lands and GitGuardian doesn't show up after ~5 min, push an empty no-op commit.
3. **Spillover-as-slice via orchestrator**. Engineer 111 didn't run tests locally before push (skipped the `cd web && npm run test:e2e` step), so the dashboard test failure surfaced in CI rather than during slice 111's session. Orchestrator filed slice 193 on the engineer's behalf per Amendment 2. Pattern matches slice 165/166/168/170/171 chain — slice 111's un-skip caught the bug exactly as designed.

Updated ready queue post-merge:

- **Slice 189** newly-ready (gate `188 merged` satisfied)
- **Slice 193** ready (spillover from 111)
- **Slices 112-115** still gated on `111 merged + 5 more clean post-111 Playwright runs` — need new soak window to accrue
- **Slices 190, 191, 192** gated on predecessors in spine chain (189 → 190 → 191 → 192)

## Drift detected — 2026-05-21 (auth-substrate-v2 spine specs filed + slice 111 unblocked)

Two unrelated queue unblocks in a single PR:

### Spine specs 188-192 filed

OQ #21 Reading D (2026-05-20) committed atlas to the OAuth Authorization Server architecture but only slice 187 (foundation) had a spec file. Slices 188-192 were sketched in 187's spec's "spine continues" notes but never written up. This PR files all 5 specs:

| #   | Title                                                      | Estimate | Key shape                                                                                                                                         |
| --- | ---------------------------------------------------------- | -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 188 | `/oauth/token` + token-exchange                            | 2-3d     | client_credentials + RFC 8693 token-exchange (tenant switch primitive) + oauth_clients table + oauth_token_exchanges audit                        |
| 189 | `/oauth/authorize` + PKCE + frontend OAuth client          | 2-3d     | RFC 7636 PKCE S256-only + auth_code redemption + open-redirect prevention via registered URI table + frontend oauth-client.ts                     |
| 190 | JWT validation middleware + revoke + introspect            | 2-3d     | CUTOVER SLICE — JWT middleware on `/v1/*` (coexists with legacy bearer); RFC 7009 + RFC 7662 endpoints; R2 = pure claim check (eventual eviction) |
| 191 | SDK migration + CLI device-code + legacy retirement        | 3-4d     | Go/Python/TS OAuth clients + RFC 8628 device-code CLI + slice 034 bearer middleware RETIRED + 410 Gone deprecation responder                      |
| 192 | Multi-tenant switch + frontend switcher (closes slice 141) | 2-3d     | `<TenantSwitcher>` dropdown + GET /v1/me/tenants + bootstrap branch + membership-removed UX banner + HIDDEN when single-tenant                    |

Each spec carries 20-36 ACs, 10-11 P0 anti-criteria, threat model, decisions log directives. Strict dep chain (188 → 189 → 190 → 191 → 192) — they pick up one at a time as predecessors merge. Slice 188 is the only one currently `ready`; the rest are `not-ready` with explicit gates.

Total spine effort: 11-16d at engineer wall-clock (typical engineer productivity multiplier: assume each takes 1-2 hours of agent wall-clock).

### Slice 111 unblocked

Slice 111's gate was "5 clean post-082 runs of `Frontend · Playwright e2e`". Live audit 2026-05-21: **56 consecutive clean Playwright runs** on main since the last failure at 2026-05-20T01:46:19Z (slice 172). The 5-clean-runs threshold is comfortably satisfied. Slice 111 row flipped `not-ready` → `ready`.

Slice 111 is a 0.25d AFK un-skip on `web/e2e/dashboard.spec.ts`. Once 111 merges, slices 112-115 unblock (each gated on "111 + 5 clean post-082 runs"); 116 caps the chain.

### Loop status

The loop hit GUARD-1 four times in a row after batches 85+86 closed (slices 187+155+173 shipped). This PR repopulates the queue with **2 newly-ready slices** (111, 188) — enough to fire the loop again.

Engineer stalled once at security-review phase (classic "review output as deliverable" pattern); orchestrator resumed with explicit ship-now directive. Post-rebase `_STATUS.md` 3-way merge resolved manually (canonical recipe documented in this drift block for future batches). 20/20 ACs · 11/11 P0 anti-criteria honored. Unblocks slices 188-192.
- **Slice 155** (Questionnaire tracer-bullet) merged at `12da637` via PR #433 · v1 questionnaire response feature locked-scope ship. Three post-rebase CI failures fixed by orchestrator: (a) `questionnaire_questions.scf_anchor_id` + `answer_library.scf_anchor_id` FK type mismatch — engineer keyed TEXT scf_id against UUID id target; relaxed to free-form TEXT for tracer-bullet (v2 spillover documented in-migration), (b) openapi-drift — added 7 RouteSpec entries + regen `docs/openapi.yaml` via `just openapi-generate`, (c) `go mod tidy` for `golang.org/x/image` transitive via excelize/v2.

Both PRs landed UNSTABLE on non-required checks only (codecov/patch informational; Self-host bundle pre-existing flake unrelated to slice scope). All required-checks PASS on both.

Process learnings (for future batches):

1. **JUDGMENT-slice stall pattern repeats** at security-review phase. The per-slice template explicitly names this stall shape but engineers continue to surface it. Slice 04's HARD RULE language is correct; the stall happens anyway. Suggests prompt drift; consider a stronger sentinel ("DELIVER PR or ESCALATE, never both") in future engineer prompts.
2. **\_STATUS.md 3-way merge recipe** is now canonical: `git checkout --ours docs/issues/_STATUS.md` then re-apply the slice's specific row flip + run `pre-commit run --files docs/issues/_STATUS.md` to settle prettier column-padding. Took ~3 minutes per slice to resolve.
3. **Migration FK type checks** should be a build-time CHECK before push. The `scf_anchor_id TEXT REFERENCES scf_anchors(id) [UUID]` would have failed locally if the engineer ran `psql -f` against a fresh DB. Consider a pre-commit hook that drains migrations against an ephemeral container for new migration files. Filing as a follow-up consideration; not a slice.

Trace-driven diagnosis run BEFORE applying a fix per the slice doc's recommended workflow. PR #368's Playwright job log (run 26115121287, job 76802322769) shows the actual error — NOT a 30s `waitForResponse` timeout (slice doc Narrative assumption), but `locator.check: Clicking the checkbox did not change its state` raised at 756ms / 731ms on retry. Engineer picks a NEW hypothesis (H4): the production input is React-controlled (`<input type="checkbox" checked={email}>` where `email = row.email !== false`), so Playwright's `.check()` post-state verification sees the checkbox snap back to `checked=false` after React re-renders with the stale `prefsQuery.data` (cache not yet invalidated). H1/H2/H3 all rejected: testids match (H1), URLs match (H2), production code DOES wire `onChange → patchMut.mutate` (H3). Slice 168 engineer's "PATCH never fires" framing was itself a misdiagnosis: the PATCH does in fact fire — the failure is in Playwright's strict post-click state verification, not the network.

| Row | Transition              | Evidence                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| --- | ----------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 171 | `ready` → `in-progress` | branch `quality/171-ac-3-patch-misfire` claim-stake commit; engineer picks H4 (controlled-checkbox post-state verification failure, not PATCH misfire); narrow ≤5-line spec fix swapping `toggle.check()` for `toggle.click()` planned |

**Counts delta:** ready −1 · in-progress +1.

## Drift detected — 2026-05-19 (slice 168 → in-progress · slice 170 filed for AC-2 spillover)

Continuous-loop pickup — slice 168 (diagnose + fix remaining 4 settings.spec.ts AC failures, Quality / JUDGMENT / 1d). Branch `quality/168-settings-4-acs` claim-staked off main at `722011b`.

Per-AC triage from the CI artifact at run 26106619396 (job 76772362534) + the trace zips in `playwright-report`:

- **AC-1 (spec drift):** `<CardTitle>Profile</CardTitle>` renders as a shadcn `<div>`, not a heading element — `getByRole("heading", { name: /Profile/ })` cannot match. Fixed in the spec by swapping to `getByText("Profile")` scoped to the profile section.
- **AC-2 (production bug):** `AppearanceSelector`'s `useState` lazy initializer is SSR-guarded — server returns `DEFAULT_THEME`, client never re-reads localStorage on hydration. Fix requires a `useEffect` (or `useSyncExternalStore` / `dynamic({ssr:false})`), beyond the single-line testid carve-out in P0-A4. **Filed as spillover slice 170; AC-2 stays red in this PR.**
- **AC-3 (test-infra gap):** `fixtures/e2e/settings.sql`'s `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` left any stale `user_notification_preferences` row from a prior test run untouched, so AC-3 opened with a CHECKED toggle and `toggle.check()` was a no-op. Fixed by upserting with `ON CONFLICT (...) DO UPDATE SET enabled = EXCLUDED.enabled`.
- **AC-4 (spec drift):** `getByRole("button", { name: /Issue token/ })` matched both the trigger button (settings-token-issue-button) AND the form submit button (page.tsx:1121-1123), raising a strict-mode violation. Fixed in the spec by scoping the role lookup to the form via `getByTestId("settings-token-issue-form").getByRole(...)`.

| Row | Transition              | Evidence                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
| --- | ----------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 168 | `ready` → `in-progress` | branch `quality/168-settings-4-acs` claim-stake commit; engineer subagent triaging 4 ACs in cost order (AC-1, AC-3, AC-4 fixed in this PR; AC-2 punted to slice 170)                                                                                                                                                              |
| 170 | (new) → `ready`         | slice doc filed at `docs/issues/170-settings-theme-picker-hydration-bug.md` during slice 168 AC-2 diagnosis · Quality · JUDGMENT · 0.25d · AC-2 production hydration bug — `useState` lazy init runs only on SSR, client never reads localStorage post-hydration · slice 168 closes AC-1 + AC-3 + AC-4; AC-2 closes via slice 170 |

**Counts delta:** ready −1 · in-progress +1 · new ready +1 (170).

CTE+LEFT-JOIN restructure of `policies.sql` + drop-redundant-casts in `scf_anchors.sql`. sqlc now emits pointer-style nullable types natively (`*int64`, `*EvidenceResult`, `*string`). ## Drift detected — 2026-05-18 (batch 61 final reconcile · 160 + 161 → merged)

Continuous-loop batch 61 complete. Both engineer PRs merged:

- Slice 160 (missing e2e fixture) — PR #342 at `f42aedf`. Tracer-bullet 0.25d AFK; fixture file added.
- Slice 161 (auth-open-redirect spec drift) — PR #343 at `f090192`. JUDGMENT 0.5d. Engineer diagnosed **Case 2 (spec drift)** — NOT a security regression. The slice 086 open-redirect defense IS functioning correctly (host assertion at line 71 passed in the failing CI run; attacker URL was rejected). Root cause: spec's `waitForURL` predicate `(url) => url.origin === new URL(authedPage.url()).origin` was self-referential — the candidate was compared against the same page's URL, so the predicate resolved immediately and `final = new URL(authedPage.url())` captured `/login?from=...` mid-redirect. 1-line fix: `(url) => !url.pathname.startsWith("/login")`. Test-the-test verified the spec REDs on the racy version.

**No security regression** — the open-redirect defense in `web/lib/safe-redirect.ts` continues to be gated by the always-required vitest (9 cases green).

## Drift detected — 2026-05-18 (batch 58 final reconcile · 146 → merged)

Continuous-loop batch 58 (solo) complete. Slice 146 (BFF cookie regression in Next.js production-build standalone) merged via PR #327 at `ca52ad9`. Engineer 146 stalled after security-review (returned "No findings to report" as final output without committing); orchestrator closed out manually (all 7 files were perfectly staged).

| Row | Transition             | Evidence                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| --- | ---------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| 146 | `in-review` → `merged` | PR gh#327 · merged `ca52ad9` · ROOT CAUSE: `signIn` server action set `sa_session_token` with `secure: process.env.NODE_ENV === "production"` → emitted `Secure` cookie attribute on every prod-build deploy → operators serving over HTTP (default for Unraid/Helm/docker-compose) had browsers refuse the cookie → BFF saw no cookie → `/api/**` redirected to `/login` → fetch parsed login HTML as JSON ("Unexpected token '<'"). FIX: new `web/lib/secure-cookie.ts::shouldUseSecureCookie` detects per-request transport via X-Forwarded-Proto / RFC 7239 Forwarded headers; defaults NOT-secure. 8 vitest cases + quarantined Playwright spec (`web/e2e/bff-cookie-production-build.spec.ts` runs against `node .next/standalone/server.js` with `ATLAS_PROD_BUILD` env) + operator runbook at `docs/runbooks/bff-cookie-forwarding.md` + decisions log at `docs/audit-log/146-bff-cookie-regression-decisions.md` (D1-D6). Security-review clean. Orchestrator close-out: engineer stalled post-security-review with all 7 files staged; manual rebase + impl commit + status flip + prettier amend + push + PR. |

**Counts delta:** in-review −1 · merged +1.

NEW LESSON LEARNED THIS BATCH (capture for future spawning prompts):

- **Engineers can stall AFTER security-review too, not just after grill-with-docs.** Engineer-146 ran security-review, returned "No findings to report" as final output, and never committed/pushed/opened PR. All 7 files were perfectly staged in the worktree. Pattern: any sub-skill that returns "report-shaped" output (grill / security-review / ship-gate / simplify) is a stall risk. Engineer prompts should explicitly say: "EVERY sub-skill output is a NO-OP CHECKPOINT, not a deliverable. Proceed straight to the next workflow step in the SAME agent turn. The PR open is the only deliverable."

7 of 8 v1.11.0 fix-slices done (146-152 + 156 + 157). LAST remaining: 153 (logo standalone) — sequential solo batch (same Next.js standalone family as 146; cannot pair).
